title: Tom Kane
content:

|name=Tom Kane

{{Quote|They played my Yoda for George and got approval, and suddenly I found myself doing Yoda for videogames, and that led to toys, and commercials. When it came time to do ''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'', somebody up north just said, 'We're just going to use Tom.'|Tom Kane|<ref name="Voice of the Force">{{InsiderCite|110|Voice of the Force}}</ref>}}
'''Tom Kane''' (born [[April 15]], [[1960s#1962|1962]])<ref name="LEGO A-Wing Starfighter" /> is a retired [[Wikipedia:Americans|American]] voice actor who had worked on many ''Star Wars'' productions, including films, television, and video games. He often served as a voice double for [[Gial Ackbar|Admiral Ackbar]], [[C-3PO]], and [[Yoda]], previously voiced by [[Erik Bauersfeld]], [[Anthony Daniels]], and [[Frank Oz]] in the films. Most notably, he had multiple recurring roles in the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series ([[2008]]–[[2020]]), not only voicing Yoda but also as the show's newsreel narrator and [[Wullf Yularen|Admiral Yularen]].

On [[December 30]], 2020, Kane's daughter, Sam disclosed that her father had suffered a left-sided stroke in [[October]] that left him with right-sided weakness and damage to the speech center of his brain. Kane has been undergoing speech, occupational, and physical therapy as treatment for his injuries. However, he has been informed by his neurologists about concerns that he may have to retire from voice-overs.<ref name="Facebook">{{Facebook|username=Tom.Kane.VO.Guy|url=Tom.Kane.VO.Guy/posts/10159336102355815|name=[[Tom Kane]]|date=December 31, 2020|archivedate=20210117045756}}</ref>
